gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: updateTokenPoolInfo lacks role check at Portal entry point, allowing unprivileged attacker to overwrite _dexPools and redirect DEX swaps through attacker-controlled pool
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.updateTokenPoolInfo (L511-522) only carries onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no role check at the Portal layer; role check (if any) deferred entirely to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall target whose source is absent from the repo
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker overwrites _dexPools[token].pool with attacker-controlled contract, then calls swapExactInput for a DEX-graduated token; attacker-controlled pool returns inflated amountOut, draining Portal BNB/token reserves

live_preconditions.name: protocol_not_halted
live_preconditions.observed_value: bitFlags not readable from live_context seed; proxy holds 1361 BNB suggesting active operation

live_preconditions.name: dex_graduated_token_exists
live_preconditions.observed_value: missing — no LaunchedToDEX event samples in live_context seed

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER_address
live_preconditions.observed_value: missing — immutable not readable from live_context seed
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast storage 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0 <slot_for_PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC  OR  grep PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER in constructor calldata of 0xeb6c62d1885c421797d0c494e693c90d4b54125a

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER_enforces_role_check
live_preconditions.observed_value: UNVERIFIED — PortalDexRouter.sol source absent from repo; IPortalDexRouter interface NatSpec at src/interfaces/IPortal.sol L1899 states 'can only be called by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE roles', strongly suggesting implementation does enforce the role; this is the critical unknown

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Portal entry point carries only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no role check at this layer; entire access-control responsibility delegated to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation

Interface NatSpec comment explicitly states 'can only be called by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE roles'; this is the strongest available evidence that the implementation DOES enforce a role check, which would block the attack entirely if confirmed
Confirms swapExactInput routes to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER when state.status == TokenStatus.DEX, reading _dexPools[token]; attacker-controlled pool value would be consumed here
_dexPools mapping is in PortalBase storage and is directly writable via delegatecall from PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER

attacker_inputs: token: address of any DEX-graduated token (status == TokenStatus.DEX)
poolInfo.pool: attacker-controlled contract returning type(uint256).max as amountOut
call_sequence: 1. portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(token, PackedDexPool({pool: attackerPool, fee: X, poolType: X, tickSpacing: X, unused: 0})) — succeeds only if PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER lacks role check

2. portal.swapExactInput({inputToken: quoteToken, outputToken: token, inputAmount: X, minOutputAmount: 0, ...}) — routes through attackerPool via _dexPools[token], attackerPool returns inflated output
state_after: Portal overpays attacker on swap; BNB or token reserves drained

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: BNB or launched tokens held by Portal proxy (1361+ BNB at captured block)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Excess tokens or BNB returned by attacker-controlled pool beyond legitimate swap output
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Portal proxy reserve depletion; other users' swaps routed through attacker pool
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker pays legitimate inputAmount but receives output computed by attacker-controlled pool returning arbitrary inflated value; no entitlement to excess

setup: Resolve PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER immutable address from Portal constructor calldata or storage
Query LaunchedToDEX events to obtain a valid DEX-graduated token address
Deploy MockPool contract that returns type(uint256).max as amountOut on any swap call
transaction_sequence: vm.prank(vm.addr(1)); portal.updateTokenPoolInfo(dexToken, PackedDexPool({pool: address(mockPool), fee: 2500, poolType: PoolType.UniV3, tickSpacing: 50, unused: 0}))
If above does not revert: assert portal._dexPools(dexToken).pool == address(mockPool)
If mapping updated: vm.prank(vm.addr(1)); portal.swapExactInput(...) and assert outputAmount > legitimate pool output
expected_assertions: REJECT path: call in step 1 reverts with AccessControl or DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE missing error — role check exists in PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER implementation
CONFIRM path: call in step 1 succeeds AND _dexPools[token].pool == mockPool AND subsequent swap returns inflated output
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If updateTokenPoolInfo reverts with any role/access-control error from within the PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER delegatecall, the attack is fully blocked and this candidate must be REJECTED

rejection_reason: Cannot be promoted to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE because the critical gate — whether PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER enforces DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE on updateTokenPoolInfo — cannot be resolved from available source. PortalDexRouter.sol is absent from the repository. The IPortalDexRouter interface NatSpec at src/interfaces/IPortal.sol L1899 explicitly documents 'can only be called by DEX_ROUTER_MANAGER_ROLE roles', which is strong evidence the implementation DOES enforce the role check and the attack is blocked. A BSC fork test is required to determine whether the call reverts or succeeds from an unprivileged caller before any further promotion.
